purpose: Bootstrap module for the image group; installs imagemagick and ffmpeg via apt as global CLI tools, and injects Pillow into ipython's pipx environment so it's available in the global REPL without polluting system Python.
invariants: imagemagick and ffmpeg are always global (apt); no Python imaging libraries are installed globally
Pillow is only injected if ipython is already installed — a warning is logged and the step is skipped otherwise
Pillow injection is idempotent: skipped with log_skip if 'import PIL' succeeds in the ipython env
Python imaging libraries (OpenCV, scikit-image, Pillow for project use) are project-local via uv — never global
gotchas: ImageMagick 7 ships the 'magick' driver binary; older versions used 'convert' — image_record_manifest checks for magick first and falls back to convert for the manifest binary field
Pillow is a library, not a CLI — it cannot go in ~/tools/bin; pipx inject is the only correct global install path for REPL use
pipx inject is not idempotent by default; the has check (ipython -c 'import PIL') gates the inject call to avoid duplicate injection
The python group (ipython) must run before the image group for Pillow injection to succeed — bootstrap.sh ordering enforces this

key_functions.name: image_pillow
key_functions.summary: Injects Pillow into the ipython pipx environment via 'pipx inject ipython Pillow' — skipped if ipython is absent or Pillow is already importable

key_functions.name: image_record_manifest
key_functions.summary: Upserts manifest entries for imagemagick (using 'magick' or 'convert' binary), ffmpeg, and Pillow (via ipython detect) if present
